title: Build supported board matrix
description: Scope: build and package the recovered supported Zephyr board/application matrix, split into children for nRF52840, STM32WL, ESP32, native_sim/apps, and Rust bridges. After .56, run with AWS_PROFILE=personal and AWS_REGION=us-west-2 in account 210337117346 on an owned Project=LICHEN builder in us-west-2c using only approved EBS vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. Each child starts from a fresh clean worktree at the recorded base, applies only its identified recovery commit/binary patch, and records source, patch, manifest, board selector, toolchain, command, output, packaging, size, log, and hash provenance. This matrix checks supported production/app builds; it does not duplicate .35.6 link_crypto, puck/L2, kiss_transport, or link-migration bridge gates.
design: Every row records exact board/SoC/app selector, pristine/full command and environment, base and applied commit IDs, patch SHA-256, west manifest, Zephyr/SDK/compiler/CMake/Ninja/Rust versions as applicable, UTC start/end, exit status, warning/error output, ELF/BIN/HEX/package SHA-256, and RAM/flash report/delta. Unsupported aliases, fallback boards, omitted variants, ignored packaging/signing failures, stale outputs, STM32WL overflow, warnings promoted by declared gates, nonzero exits, or dirty-worktree verification fail closed. Evidence is copied and hashed before cleanup; then sync, verify no mount users, unmount /mnt/lichen-zephyr, ownership-check exact RunId/instance/volume, detach vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb, wait for volume-available, and terminate only the owned tagged instance.
acceptance_criteria: All five children pass every declared build/package row with exact selectors and no silent matrix reduction. nRF52840 covers T-Echo, RAK4631, T1000-E, R1 Neo where supported, and lora_ping Renode overlays; STM32WL covers Nucleo WL55JC Puck/Gateway/lora_ping and memory limits; ESP32 covers T-Deck and supported Heltec ESP32-S3 variants; native_sim/apps covers Puck/Gateway native_sim and native_sim/native/64; Rust bridge child covers declared ESP32/nRF52840/STM32WL bridge targets without duplicating .35.6 link gates. Source/patch/tool/command/log/artifact hashes and RAM/flash deltas are retained; every failure is fatal. Parent closes only after all children and mandatory EBS unmount/detach/wait-available plus ownership-checked cleanup succeed.
notes: Preserved original board intent: T-Echo, T1000-E, R1 Neo, T-Deck, Heltec ESP32-S3, RAK4631, Nucleo WL55JC, native_sim applications, lora_ping overlays, and declared Rust bridges. Record exact blockers per target rather than treating an unsupported alias or memory overflow as a pass.
